title: feat: Purpose Code Display
description:
Each location has an ARR and DEP purpose code (corresponding to the ARR/DEP event for that location.  This should be displayed in the Per ICAO section heading of checklists instances.

The section title is currently set on line 419 of src/screens/ChecklistView/index.jsx.  It only displays the DEP PC and LABEL. Change the display to show the ARR PC and LABEL. It should be formatted as 
Section Title - SD.DEP_LOC  → SD.ARR_PC · SD.ARR_PC_LABEL SD.ARR_LOC  SD.DEP_PC · SD.DEP_PC_LABEL

The PC and its label should be in a badge the way it currently is.

title: feat: custom placeholders

Allowing custom placeholders inside custom placeholders should be allowed, the order of operations will be top to bottom in the list. This means the list must be able to be ordered by the user.

log.text: The custom placeholder should be available anywhere built-in placeholders are available in a "User Defined" section
If during placeholder resolve a custom placeholder references a custom placeholder that is not resolved already just leave the placeholder in its position and unchanged as if it were regular text

title: feat: update itinerary for existing checklist instance
description: New itinerary modal similar to new instance modal but only the load itinerary section. Load the itinerary but check to ensure the stop order is the same. Only accept it if everything is the same EXCEPT times can be different

log.text: Once the itinerary is validated and loaded rerun the placeholder resolver. Do not re-apply the checklist, change item states or remove anything. just update the placeholders. also update the times shown on the section headers. Also if the cargo or hazmat is changed for a MSN, do the same reprocessing procedures as a new itin.

title: feat: JSON template file size handling
description:
Every time any template is touched, the entire array (including every other template's Excel blob) gets rewritten to disk and re-POSTed. If you have 20 large templates and edit a field mapping on template #1, you're still writing all 20 templates' worth of binary data.

This is a structural issue with the current single-array-per-key model.

For your usage scale (single operator, ≤20 templates), this is fine. The whole thing is async and invisible.

If it ever becomes a concern, the fix is clean — split binary file data out of the JSON entirely:

mcm_form_templates.json     ← metadata only (name, mappings, ids) — tiny
mcm_form_template_files/    ← mcm_form_template_<id>.bin — one file per template

log.text: is this still an inssue with IndexDB transition?
